name: Chocolate Mousse Cake with Raspberry Eau De Vie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup unsalted butter, cut into pieces
3/4 pound semisweet or bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine 1/2 cup sugar and water. Bring to boil over low heat, stirring occasionally to ensure all sugar crystals dissolve.
2. Remove from heat. Stir in butter and chocolate. Cover pan. Let stand for 5 minutes. Whisk smooth.
3. In a bowl, whisk eau de vie and eggs until well combined. In a steady stream, whisk in chocolate mixture, being careful not to overmix.
4. Pour batter into a buttered 8-inch round cake pan lined with buttered parchment or wax paper.
5. Pour 1 inch warm water into a small roasting pan. Set the cake pan in the water. Bake at 300°F on the middle oven rack until the cake is set and slightly dry on surface, about 45 minutes.
6. Remove cake from roasting pan and cool in pan to room temperature. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
7. To unmold, run a knife between dessert and pan. Immerse bottom of pan in hot water. Invert onto serving platter.
8. To prepare frosting, whip cream with 2 tablespoons sugar until soft peaks form. Spread on top of dessert. Edge with raspberries.
Each serving contains about:
512 calories; 41 mg sodium; 167 mg cholesterol; 39 grams fat; 13 grams carbohydrates; 3 grams protein; 0 fiber.
